overview.text: The New South Wales Police Force is the primary law-enforcement agency for NSW. It is one of the largest police organisations in the English-speaking world, providing 24/7 emergency response, criminal investigation, traffic policing, public-order management and specialist operations across the state.

items: Established in 1862 as the unified NSW Police Force.
Around 17,000 sworn officers and 4,000 administrative staff.
Operates from roughly 470 stations grouped into Police Area Commands (metro) and Police Districts (regional).
Specialist units include PolAir, Marine Area Command, Counter Terrorism, Dog Unit, Rescue & Bomb Disposal, Tactical Operations Unit, Negotiators, Public Order & Riot Squad.
Routine NSWPF radio traffic on the PSN is heavily encrypted; many talkgroups carry minimal cleartext.
Some specialist and tasking channels remain in clear or partially in clear.
Be aware that publishing anything that could compromise an active operation or identify victims/officers is illegal under NSW law.
